title: Rich Smoky Australian Morbier Gratin.
description: A rich, molten, Australian dish featuring morbier cheese, smoke-cured meats, and a crunchy, sugar-free-friendly crust.

ingredients.name: Morbier cheese
ingredients.notes: A mild, creamy French cheese with a subtle sweetness.

ingredients.name: Smoked lamb sausage
ingredients.notes: Smoked to perfection to add a deep, rich flavor.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Thinly sliced to caramelise quickly and add sweetness.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince to release its natural oils and intensify the flavor.

ingredients.name: Thyme
ingredients.notes: Fresh or dried, to add a herbaceous, slightly minty flavor.

ingredients.name: Smoked bacon
ingredients.notes: Crumbled to add a smoky, salty flavor.

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: To bind the filling together and add richness.

ingredients.name: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: To thicken the filling and create a crispy crust.

ingredients.name: Unsalted butter
ingredients.notes: To grease the dish and create a golden-brown crust.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: To enhance the flavors and balance the sweetness.

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: To add a bright, fresh flavor and color.

instructions: Grease a 20cm (8in) round baking dish with unsalted butter.
Thinly slice one onion and sauté it in a pan until caramelised, stirring occasionally.
Mince three cloves of garlic and add it to the pan, sautéing for one minute or until fragrant.
Add the smoked lamb sausage to the pan, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the thyme sprigs to the pan and stir to combine.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked sausage mixture, morbier cheese, crumbled smoked bacon, and egg.
Season the mixture with salt to taste.
Pour the mixture into the prepared baking dish and smooth the top.
Dust the top of the dish with all-purpose flour.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes or until the top is golden brown and the filling is set.
Remove the gratin from the oven and sprinkle with fresh parsley.
